subroutines.name: release_disc_space
subroutines.title: Release disc space back to free space map
subroutines.description:
Return the disc space occupied by the object at
wksp_object_sector (3 bytes) with size at &1037-&1039
(3 bytes) back to the free space map. Searches for the
correct position in the sorted FSM and merges with
adjacent free entries where possible.

subroutines.name: check_filename_length
subroutines.title: Check filename is within 10-character limit
subroutines.description:
Scan up to 10 characters of filename at (&B4),Y. Raises
Bad name error if no terminator found within 10 characters.
Then copies the directory entry name to the object name
workspace.

subroutines.name: compare_filename
subroutines.title: Compare filename against pattern with wildcards
subroutines.description:
Compare the object name in workspace against the pattern
at (&B4),Y. Supports '#' (match one char) and '*' (match
rest) wildcards. Case-insensitive comparison.

On entry:
  Object name in wksp_object_name (10 bytes)
  (&B4),Y points to pattern string
  X = index into object name
On exit:
  Z set if match, Z clear if no match

subroutines.name: claim_nmi
subroutines.title: Claim NMI via service call 12
subroutines.description:
Issue service call 12 (NMI claim) via OSBYTE &8F to claim
exclusive use of the NMI handler for floppy disc operations.
Saves the return argument for later release.

subroutines.name: release_nmi
subroutines.title: Release NMI via service call 11
subroutines.description:
Issue service call 11 (NMI released) via OSBYTE &8F to
release the NMI handler after floppy disc operations.

subroutines.name: floppy_init_transfer
subroutines.title: Initialise floppy disc transfer
subroutines.description:
Set up for a floppy disc operation: clear error number,
copy the transfer address and control parameters from
the control block, claim NMI, set step rate, and copy
the NMI handler code to NMI workspace.

subroutines.name: apply_head_load_flag
subroutines.title: Apply head load delay to FDC command
subroutines.description:
If the head-loaded flag is set in the transfer state,
OR bit 2 into A (the head load delay bit in WD1770
step/seek commands).

subroutines.name: floppy_format_track
subroutines.title: Format a floppy disc track
subroutines.description:
Set up NMI handler addresses for a format operation,
then write the track format data to disc.

subroutines.name: fdc_write_register_verify
subroutines.title: Write to WD1770 register with readback verify
subroutines.description:
Write value from zp_a3+X to FDC register at &FE85+X,
then read back and loop until the value matches.
This handles the WD1770's register write timing.

subroutines.name: print_entry_info
subroutines.title: Print full catalogue info for one directory entry
subroutines.description:
Print a directory entry in *INFO/*EX format:
  filename  access/  loadaddr execaddr length sector [D]

Entry at (&B6) is a 26-byte directory entry. Checks the
E (execute-only) attribute and suppresses detail if set.
Uses 3-byte addresses for small files, 4-byte for large.

subroutines.name: check_special_dir_char
subroutines.title: Check for ^ (parent) or @ (current) directory
subroutines.description:
Check if the first character of the argument is ^ (parent
directory) or @ (current directory). Sets (&B6) to point
to the appropriate directory footer area.

On exit:
  Z set if ^ or @ matched, (&B6) set accordingly
  Z clear if neither matched

subroutines.name: parse_dir_argument
subroutines.title: Parse optional directory path argument
subroutines.description:
If a directory argument is given, parse the path and load
the target directory. If no argument, use the current
directory (checking it's initialised first).

Used by *EX, *CAT, *CDIR, and *DIR.

subroutines.name: switch_to_library
subroutines.title: Switch CSD to library directory
subroutines.description:
Save the current CSD sector address, then replace it with
the library directory sector address. Used before *LCAT
and *LEX to temporarily operate on the library.

subroutines.name: restore_csd
subroutines.title: Restore CSD sector from saved copy
subroutines.description:
Restore the CSD sector address from the temporary save
in wksp_1030. Used after *LCAT/*LEX to switch back.

subroutines.name: calc_total_free_space
subroutines.title: Calculate total free space on disc
subroutines.description:
Sum all free space entries in the FSM to get the total
free space. Prepares workspace for display by *FREE.

On exit:
  3-byte sum in wksp_disc_op_result (little-endian)

subroutines.name: print_space_value
subroutines.title: Print space value in hex and decimal
subroutines.description:
Print a 3-byte space value from the disc op workspace
as hex bytes followed by ' Sectors = NNN Bytes'.
Used by *FREE for displaying free and used space.

subroutines.name: copy_default_dir_name
subroutines.title: Copy default directory name to workspace
subroutines.description:
Copy the reversed string 'Unset' (with quotes and CR
padding) to the CSD or library name workspace at &1100+X.
Used when dismounting or initialising to set the directory
name to the default 'Unset' value.

On entry:
  X = offset into workspace (0 for CSD, 10 for library)

subroutines.name: parse_drive_argument
subroutines.title: Parse optional drive number argument
subroutines.description:
Parse an optional drive number from the command line for
commands like *DISMOUNT, *MOUNT, *FREE, *MAP. If no
argument given, uses the current drive.

subroutines.name: check_compaction_recommended
subroutines.title: Check if disc compaction is recommended
subroutines.description:
After *MAP output, check if the FSM has become fragmented
enough to recommend compaction. Prints a recommendation
message if the free space list pointer exceeds &E1.

subroutines.name: help_print_header
subroutines.title: Print *HELP version header line
subroutines.description:
Print a newline followed by the ROM version string for
*HELP output. Uses print_inline_string.

subroutines.name: skip_filename
subroutines.title: Skip past filename in command string
subroutines.description:
Advance (&B4) past the next filename component in the
command string, handling dots as path separators.

subroutines.name: skip_spaces
subroutines.title: Skip leading spaces in command argument
subroutines.description:
Advance (&B4) past leading spaces. Also handles
double-quoted strings (skips to closing quote).

On exit:
  (&B4) points past the skipped characters

subroutines.name: check_drive_colon
subroutines.title: Check for drive specifier colon
subroutines.description:
Check if the next character at (&B4) is a colon,
indicating a drive number follows.

On exit:
  Z set if no colon found
  If colon found, jumps to parse drive number

subroutines.name: verify_dir_integrity
subroutines.title: Verify directory buffer integrity
subroutines.description:
Check that the directory buffer contains a valid directory
by verifying the Hugo identity string and master sequence
number are consistent at both ends of the directory.
Raises Broken directory error if verification fails.

subroutines.name: claim_tube
subroutines.title: Claim Tube if present
subroutines.description:
Claim the Tube for a data transfer if a Tube is present.

On entry:
  (&B0),Y points to the 4-byte Tube transfer address
  zp_flags bit 7 set if Tube is present
On exit:
  Tube claimed and transfer address copied to workspace
  zp_flags bit 6 set to indicate Tube in use

subroutines.name: release_tube
subroutines.title: Release Tube if in use
subroutines.description:
Release the Tube after a data transfer if it was claimed.

On entry:
  zp_flags bit 6 set if Tube is in use
On exit:
  Tube released
  zp_flags bit 6 cleared

subroutines.name: scsi_get_status
subroutines.title: Read SCSI status with settling
subroutines.description:
Read the SCSI status register, waiting for the value to settle.
Reads the status twice and loops until consecutive reads match.

On exit:
  A = settled SCSI status byte
  zp_scsi_status = same value

subroutines.name: scsi_start_command
subroutines.title: SCSI bus selection and command phase
subroutines.description:
Select a SCSI device on the bus and begin the command phase.
Asserts the target's SCSI ID on the data bus and waits for
the BSY signal to be asserted by the target.

subroutines.name: command_set_retries
subroutines.title: Set retry count for disc operation
subroutines.description: Set the retry counter to the default value (16).

subroutines.name: command_exec_xy
subroutines.title: Execute disc command with control block at (X,Y)
subroutines.description:
Execute a disc operation using the control block pointed to
by X (low) and Y (high). Handles both hard drive (SCSI) and
floppy disc operations with retry logic.

subroutines.name: generate_error
subroutines.title: Generate a BRK error
subroutines.description: Generate a BRK error from an error number and message.

subroutines.name: service_call_handler
subroutines.title: ROM service call handler
subroutines.description:
Main entry point for MOS service calls. Dispatches to
individual handlers based on the service call number in A.

subroutines.name: my_fscv
subroutines.title: Filing system control vector handler
subroutines.description:
Handle filing system control calls via FSCV. Dispatches
star commands, *RUN, *CAT, etc.

subroutines.name: my_osfile
subroutines.title: OSFILE handler
subroutines.description:
Handle OSFILE calls for whole-file operations: load, save,
read/write catalogue info, delete, create.

subroutines.name: my_osargs
subroutines.title: OSARGS handler
subroutines.description:
Handle OSARGS calls for reading and writing file arguments
(PTR, EXT, allocation) and filing system information.

subroutines.name: my_osbget
subroutines.title: OSBGET handler
subroutines.description: Handle OSBGET calls to read a single byte from an open file.

subroutines.name: my_osbput
subroutines.title: OSBPUT handler
subroutines.description: Handle OSBPUT calls to write a single byte to an open file.

subroutines.name: my_osfind
subroutines.title: OSFIND handler
subroutines.description: Handle OSFIND calls to open and close files for byte access.

subroutines.name: my_osgbpb
subroutines.title: OSGBPB handler
subroutines.description: Handle OSGBPB calls for reading and writing groups of bytes.

subroutines.name: hd_command
subroutines.title: Execute hard drive SCSI command
subroutines.description:
Execute a disc operation via the SCSI hard drive interface.
Sends the SCSI command bytes from the control block at (&B0),
performs data transfer (direct or via Tube), and reads the
SCSI status and message phases.

Falls back to floppy if drive bit 7 is set.

On entry:
  (&B0) points to the control block
  Y = 6 (index into control block)
On exit:
  A = 0 on success, error code on failure
  X, Y = control block address (restored)

subroutines.name: command_done
subroutines.title: Complete SCSI command and read status
subroutines.description:
Release the Tube, then read the SCSI status and message
bytes to determine the outcome of the command.

On exit:
  A = 0 on success, &7F-masked error code on failure
  X, Y = control block address (restored)

subroutines.name: hd_data_transfer_256
subroutines.title: SCSI 256-byte sector data transfer
subroutines.description:
Transfer complete 256-byte sectors between SCSI bus and
memory (direct or via Tube). Optimised inner loop with no
per-byte SCSI REQ polling.

On entry:
  (&B2),Y points to memory buffer
  zp_flags bit 6 indicates Tube in use

subroutines.name: scsi_request_sense
subroutines.title: SCSI Request Sense command
subroutines.description:
Send a SCSI Request Sense command (opcode 3) to retrieve
extended error information after a failed operation. Stores
the 4-byte sense data in the error workspace.

On exit:
  A = error code from sense data, or &FF if unrecoverable
  Error sector and code stored in workspace

subroutines.name: scsi_send_cmd_byte
subroutines.title: Send one byte during SCSI command phase
subroutines.description: Wait for SCSI REQ, then write byte A to the SCSI data bus.

subroutines.name: wait_ensuring
subroutines.title: Wait while files are being ensured
subroutines.description:
If zp_flags bit 0 (ensuring) is set, loop until it clears.
This prevents disc operations during file ensure operations.

subroutines.name: scsi_wait_for_req
subroutines.title: Wait for SCSI REQ signal
subroutines.description:
Poll the SCSI status register until the REQ bit is asserted,
indicating the target is ready for the next bus phase.

On exit:
  A = SCSI status byte
  N flag reflects C/D bit (command/data phase)
  V flag reflects MSG bit (message phase)

subroutines.name: scsi_send_byte_a
subroutines.title: Send byte A on SCSI bus after REQ
subroutines.description:
Wait for SCSI REQ then write A to the SCSI data register.
Used during SCSI command phase to send command bytes.

subroutines.name: reload_fsm_and_dir_then_brk
subroutines.title: Reload FSM and directory then raise error
subroutines.description:
Reload the free space map and current directory from disc,
then generate a BRK error. Used after operations that may
have left the in-memory copies inconsistent.

subroutines.name: generate_error_no_suffix
subroutines.title: Generate error without drive/sector suffix
subroutines.description:
Generate a BRK error from the disc error code without
appending the drive:sector suffix.

subroutines.name: generate_error_suffix_x
subroutines.title: Generate error with suffix control in X
subroutines.description:
Generate a BRK error from the disc error code. X controls
whether the drive:sector suffix is appended.

subroutines.name: invalidate_fsm_and_dir
subroutines.title: Mark FSM and directory as invalid
subroutines.description:
Set flags to indicate that the in-memory free space map and
directory buffer may be stale and need reloading from disc.

subroutines.name: check_dir_loaded
subroutines.title: Ensure current directory is loaded
subroutines.description:
Check that the current directory buffer contains valid data.
If not, reload it from disc.

subroutines.name: get_wksp_addr_ba
subroutines.title: Get workspace address into &BA
subroutines.description: Load a workspace address into zero page locations &BA-&BB.

subroutines.name: calc_wksp_checksum
subroutines.title: Calculate workspace checksum
subroutines.description:
Calculate a checksum over the workspace area for integrity
checking.

subroutines.name: check_wksp_checksum
subroutines.title: Verify workspace checksum
subroutines.description:
Check the workspace checksum matches the stored value.
Raises a Bad checksum error if verification fails.

subroutines.name: load_fsm
subroutines.title: Load free space map from disc
subroutines.description:
Read sectors 0 and 1 from the current drive into the free
space map workspace at &0E00-&0FFF. Validates the checksum.

subroutines.name: hex_number_error_100_y
subroutines.title: Parse hex number or raise error
subroutines.description:
Parse a hexadecimal number from the command line. Raises
an error if the number is invalid.

subroutines.name: hex_digit
subroutines.title: Convert 4-bit value to ASCII hex digit
subroutines.description:
Convert a 4-bit value in A to an ASCII hex character
('0'-'9' or 'A'-'F'). The low nibble of A is used.

subroutines.name: dec_number_error_100_y
subroutines.title: Parse decimal number or raise error
subroutines.description:
Parse a decimal number from the command line. Raises an
error if the number is invalid.

subroutines.name: service_handler_1
subroutines.title: Service 1: absolute workspace claim
subroutines.description:
Initialise ADFS on a ROM filing system init service call.
Checks for floppy and hard drive hardware. If either is
present, claims the ROM workspace slot and raises PAGE
to make room for ADFS workspace.

subroutines.name: service_handler_2
subroutines.title: Service 2: private workspace claim
subroutines.description:
Claim private workspace pages. On hard break, initialises
the workspace with default values (CSD name, directory
sector pointers, checksum). On soft break, preserves
existing workspace. Sets up the filing system vectors
and checks for Tube presence.

subroutines.name: service_handler_3
subroutines.title: Service 3: auto-boot
subroutines.description:
Handle auto-boot on power-on or Ctrl+Break. Scans the
keyboard for Shift+Break (floppy boot) or A+Break
(hard drive boot). Selects ADFS as the filing system
and executes the boot file if configured.

subroutines.name: service_handler_4
subroutines.title: Service 4: unrecognised star command
subroutines.description:
Handle unrecognised star commands passed to filing system
ROMs. Matches commands against the ADFS command table
and dispatches to the appropriate handler.

subroutines.name: service_handler_8
subroutines.title: Service 8: unrecognised OSWORD
subroutines.description:
Handle unrecognised OSWORD calls. ADFS claims OSWORD &72
for direct disc access.

subroutines.name: service_handler_9
subroutines.title: Service 9: *HELP
subroutines.description:
Handle *HELP requests. Prints ADFS version information
when *HELP ADFS is entered.

subroutines.name: oscli_at_x
subroutines.title: Execute OSCLI with string at X
subroutines.description: Call OSCLI with the command string address in X (low byte).

subroutines.name: check_char_is_terminator
subroutines.title: Check if character is a filename terminator
subroutines.description:
Test whether the character at (&B4),Y is a filename
terminator: space, dot, double-quote, or control character.

On entry:
  (&B4),Y points to character to test
On exit:
  X = 0 if terminator (dot, quote, or control char)
  C set if printable non-terminator (>= space)
  C clear if control character

subroutines.name: floppy_partial_sector
subroutines.title: Floppy disc partial sector transfer
subroutines.description:
Transfer a partial sector (less than 256 bytes) to or from
a floppy disc. Used for operations that don't align to
sector boundaries.

subroutines.name: hd_command_partial_sector
subroutines.title: Hard drive partial sector transfer
subroutines.description: Transfer a partial sector via the SCSI hard drive interface.

subroutines.name: check_open
subroutines.title: Check if file is open
subroutines.description:
Check whether any files are currently open on a given drive.
Used before operations that would be unsafe with open files.

subroutines.name: star_remove
subroutines.title: *REMOVE command handler
subroutines.description:
Remove a file from the current directory. Unlike *DELETE,
*REMOVE does not report an error if the file is locked.

subroutines.name: star_ex
subroutines.title: *EX command handler
subroutines.description:
Display a full catalogue of the current or specified
directory, showing filename, attributes, load and execution
addresses, length, and start sector for each entry.

subroutines.name: star_info
subroutines.title: *INFO command handler
subroutines.description:
Display catalogue information for a single file, with the
same format as *EX but for one file only. Supports wildcards.

subroutines.name: star_dir
subroutines.title: *DIR command handler
subroutines.description:
Change the currently selected directory. With no argument,
selects the root directory of the current drive.

subroutines.name: star_cdir
subroutines.title: *CDIR command handler
subroutines.description:
Create a new directory. Allocates 5 contiguous sectors on
disc and initialises the directory structure with the Hugo
identifier, title, and parent pointer.

subroutines.name: star_access
subroutines.title: *ACCESS command handler
subroutines.description:
Change the access attributes of a file. Attributes are
specified as a combination of L (locked), W (write), R (read),
D (directory), and E (execute).

subroutines.name: star_destroy
subroutines.title: *DESTROY command handler
subroutines.description:
Delete multiple files matching a wildcard specification.
Prompts for confirmation before deleting.

subroutines.name: jmp_indirect_fscv
subroutines.title: Jump through FSCV indirect vector
subroutines.description: Jump indirectly through the filing system control vector.

subroutines.name: hd_init_detect
subroutines.title: Detect hard drive hardware
subroutines.description:
Check whether a SCSI hard drive is present by attempting
to read the SCSI status register.

On exit:
  Z flag set if hard drive present
  Z flag clear if not present
  X, Y preserved
  A corrupted

subroutines.name: star_cmd
subroutines.title: Parse and dispatch star command
subroutines.description:
Match the command string at (&B4) against the command table
at tbl_commands. The table encodes command names with their
dispatch addresses. Supports abbreviation with dot.

Uses RTS-trick dispatch to the matched command handler.

subroutines.name: star_free
subroutines.title: *FREE command handler
subroutines.description:
Display the free space remaining on the current or specified
drive, in bytes and as a number of sectors.

subroutines.name: star_map
subroutines.title: *MAP command handler
subroutines.description:
Display the free space map of the current or specified drive,
showing the address and length of each free space region.

subroutines.name: star_delete
subroutines.title: *DELETE command handler
subroutines.description:
Delete a file from the current directory. Reports an error
if the file is locked.

subroutines.name: star_bye
subroutines.title: *BYE command handler
subroutines.description:
Close all open files and dismount all drives. Equivalent
to *CLOSE followed by *DISMOUNT for all drives.

subroutines.name: star_dismount
subroutines.title: *DISMOUNT command handler
subroutines.description:
Close all open files on the specified drive and mark the
drive as not mounted.

subroutines.name: star_mount
subroutines.title: *MOUNT command handler
subroutines.description:
Mount a drive by loading its free space map and root
directory into memory.

subroutines.name: star_title
subroutines.title: *TITLE command handler
subroutines.description:
Change the title of the currently selected directory. The
title may be up to 19 characters long.

subroutines.name: star_compact
subroutines.title: *COMPACT command handler
subroutines.description:
Compact the free space on a drive by moving files to
consolidate fragmented free space into a single contiguous
region.

subroutines.name: star_run
subroutines.title: *RUN command handler
subroutines.description:
Load and execute a file. Sets the execution address from the
file's catalogue entry.

subroutines.name: star_lib
subroutines.title: *LIB command handler
subroutines.description:
Change the current library directory. The library is searched
for commands not found in the current directory.

subroutines.name: star_lcat
subroutines.title: *LCAT command handler
subroutines.description: Display a catalogue of the current library directory.

subroutines.name: star_lex
subroutines.title: *LEX command handler
subroutines.description:
Display a full catalogue of the current library directory,
with the same format as *EX.

subroutines.name: star_back
subroutines.title: *BACK command handler
subroutines.description:
Switch the current directory to the previously selected
directory and vice versa.

subroutines.name: star_rename
subroutines.title: *RENAME command handler
subroutines.description:
Rename a file or move it between directories on the same
drive. The source and destination must be on the same drive.

subroutines.name: star_copy
subroutines.title: *COPY command handler
subroutines.description:
Copy a file. The source and destination may be on different
drives.

subroutines.name: star_close
subroutines.title: *CLOSE command handler
subroutines.description: Close all open files on all drives.

subroutines.name: print_inline_string
subroutines.title: Print bit-7-terminated inline string
subroutines.description:
Pop the return address from the stack, print the inline
string that follows the JSR instruction. Characters are
printed via OSASCI until a byte with bit 7 set is found
(the last character, printed with bit 7 stripped). Pushes
the address past the string so RTS continues after it.

subroutines.name: hd_command_bget_bput_sector
subroutines.title: Hard drive single sector for BGET/BPUT
subroutines.description:
Read or write a single sector via the SCSI interface for
byte-level file access (BGET/BPUT channel operations).

subroutines.name: check_set_channel_y
subroutines.title: Validate and set channel number from Y
subroutines.description:
Check that Y contains a valid file handle and set the
channel offset workspace variable.

subroutines.name: compare_ext_to_ptr
subroutines.title: Compare file EXT to PTR
subroutines.description:
Compare the file extent (EXT) with the current pointer
(PTR) for the channel in the workspace.

subroutines.name: fsc6_new_filing_system
subroutines.title: FSC 6: new filing system selected
subroutines.description:
Handle the FSC 6 call which notifies ADFS that a new
filing system is being selected. Ensures all files are
closed and workspace is saved.

subroutines.name: do_floppy_scsi_command_ind
subroutines.title: Floppy disc command (indirect entry)
subroutines.description:
Indirect entry point for floppy disc operations.
Jumps through to do_floppy_scsi_command.

subroutines.name: floppy_check_present
subroutines.title: Check floppy disc hardware present
subroutines.description:
Test whether the WD1770 floppy disc controller is present
by probing its registers.

On exit:
  C set if floppy hardware present
  C clear if not present

subroutines.name: do_floppy_scsi_command
subroutines.title: Execute floppy disc command
subroutines.description:
Execute a disc operation on the floppy disc using the
WD1770 controller. Handles sector read, write, and
format operations.

subroutines.name: floppy_get_step_rate
subroutines.title: Get floppy step rate
subroutines.description:
Determine the step rate for the floppy disc drive from
the workspace settings.

subroutines.name: copy_code_to_nmi_space
subroutines.title: Copy NMI handler code to NMI workspace
subroutines.description:
Copy the NMI handler routine from ROM to the NMI workspace
at &0D00. The NMI handler is used for byte-by-byte data
transfer between the WD1770 and memory.

subroutines.name: floppy_wait_nmi_finish
subroutines.title: Wait for floppy NMI transfer to complete
subroutines.description:
Wait for the WD1770 floppy disc controller to complete
a data transfer. Polls the controller status register.

subroutines.name: floppy_set_side_1
subroutines.title: Select floppy disc side 1
subroutines.description:
Select side 1 (the second side) of a double-sided floppy
disc by setting the appropriate control register bit.

subroutines.name: floppy_restore_track_0
subroutines.title: Seek floppy head to track 0
subroutines.description:
Issue a restore command to the WD1770 to seek the
read/write head to track 0.

subroutines.name: floppy_calc_track_sector_from_block_check_range
subroutines.title: Calculate track/sector from block with range check
subroutines.description:
Convert a logical block number to a physical track and
sector number for the floppy disc, checking that the
block is within the valid range for the disc.

subroutines.name: floppy_calc_track_sector_from_b0_block
subroutines.title: Calculate track/sector from block at &B0
subroutines.description:
Convert the logical block number at (&B0) to a physical
track and sector number.

subroutines.name: xa_div_16_to_ya
subroutines.title: Divide X:A by 16, result in Y:A
subroutines.description:
Divide the 16-bit value X:A by 16 (shift right 4 places).
Result quotient in Y:A, remainder lost.

subroutines.name: floppy_error
subroutines.title: Handle floppy disc error
subroutines.description:
Process an error from the WD1770 floppy disc controller.
Translates the controller error code into an ADFS error
code and stores the error information in workspace.

subroutines.name: tube_start_xfer_sei
subroutines.title: Start Tube transfer with interrupts disabled
subroutines.description:
Disable interrupts then call the Tube host code at &0406
to initiate a data transfer.

subroutines.name: tube_start_xfer
subroutines.title: Start Tube transfer
subroutines.description:
Call the Tube host code at &0406 to initiate a data transfer.
Followed by a delay for Tube synchronisation.

subroutines.name: save_wksp_and_return
subroutines.title: Save workspace state and return result
subroutines.description:
Restore the original drive if changed, reload the FSM,
restore alternative workspace if set, and save workspace
with checksum. Return value passed via A on stack.

subroutines.name: write_dir_and_validate
subroutines.title: Write directory and FSM back to disc
subroutines.description:
Verify directory integrity, validate the free space map
checksums, then write the current directory and FSM
sectors back to disc.

subroutines.name: find_first_matching_entry
subroutines.title: Find first matching directory entry
subroutines.description:
Parse a filename from the command line and search the
current directory for the first entry matching the
parsed filename pattern.

On exit:
  Z set if found, (&B6) points to matching entry
  Z clear if not found

subroutines.name: exec_disc_op_from_wksp
subroutines.title: Execute disc command from workspace control block
subroutines.description:
Execute a disc command using the control block at &1015.
Generates a BRK error if the command fails.

subroutines.name: advance_dir_entry_ptr
subroutines.title: Advance to next matching directory entry
subroutines.description:
Advance (&B6) by 26 bytes to the next directory entry,
then check whether it matches the current search pattern.

On exit:
  Z set if match found, (&B6) points to it
  Z clear if end of directory reached

subroutines.name: print_hex_byte
subroutines.title: Print a byte as two hex digits
subroutines.description:
Print the value in A as two hexadecimal ASCII digits
via OSWRCH, high nibble first.

subroutines.name: generate_disc_error
subroutines.title: Generate disc error with state recovery
subroutines.description:
Save the current drive state, reload FSM and directory,
then generate a BRK error. The inline error number and
message string follow the JSR instruction.

subroutines.name: mark_directory_dirty
subroutines.title: Validate FSM checksums and mark directory dirty
subroutines.description:
Validate the in-memory free space map by checking both
sector checksums. Generates a Bad FS map error if the
checksums do not match.

subroutines.name: not_found_error
subroutines.title: Generate Not found error
subroutines.description:
Check for special directory characters in path and
generate either Bad name or Not found error.

subroutines.name: get_object_type_result
subroutines.title: Load object type and save workspace
subroutines.description:
Load object type from workspace and fall through to
save_wksp_and_return.

On exit:
  A = object type (0=not found, 1=file, 2=directory)

subroutines.name: multi_sector_disc_command
subroutines.title: Execute multi-sector disc command
subroutines.description:
Set up sector count and execute a disc read or write
command. Rounds up partial counts for writes.

On exit:
  Z set on success

subroutines.name: exec_disc_and_check_error
subroutines.title: Search for non-directory file
subroutines.description:
Parse a filename and search the current directory for
a matching non-directory entry.

On exit:
  Z set if found, (&B6) points to entry
  Z clear if not found

subroutines.name: check_file_not_open
subroutines.title: Check file is not locked or open
subroutines.description:
Check the entry at (&B6) for the locked attribute and
generate a Locked error if set. Then check whether any
files on the current drive are open.

subroutines.name: allocate_disc_space
subroutines.title: Allocate disc space from free space map
subroutines.description:
Find the best-fit free entry for the requested size at
&103D-&103F. Generates Disc full or Compaction required
errors if allocation is not possible.

subroutines.name: osbyte_y_ff_x_00
subroutines.title: Call OSBYTE to read current value
subroutines.description:
Call OSBYTE with Y=&FF and X=0 to read the current
value of the variable specified in A.

subroutines.name: validate_not_locked
subroutines.title: Validate file is not locked then create entry
subroutines.description:
Check file is not locked or open, write the filename
into the directory entry, allocate disc space, and copy
the file length and sector address.

subroutines.name: advance_text_ptr
subroutines.title: Advance text pointer by one character
subroutines.description:
Increment the 16-bit text pointer at (&B4) by one,
handling page crossing.

subroutines.name: parse_and_setup_search
subroutines.title: Parse argument and set up directory search
subroutines.description:
Skip leading spaces, set up directory search state,
and clear the search result workspace.

subroutines.name: parse_drive_from_ascii
subroutines.title: Parse drive number from ASCII character
subroutines.description:
Convert ASCII drive character ('0'-'7' or 'A'-'H')
to a 3-bit drive ID in bits 5-7 of A. Limits to
drives 0-3 if no hard drive present.

subroutines.name: parse_filename_from_cmdline
subroutines.title: Parse filename from command line
subroutines.description:
Parse a filename from (&B4) including drive specifier,
root, and parent directory references.

On exit:
  Z set if found, (&B6) points to entry
  Z clear if not found

subroutines.name: save_text_ptr_after_match
subroutines.title: Save text pointer and determine object type
subroutines.description:
After a directory entry match, save the remaining text
position and determine whether the entry is a file
(type 1) or directory (type 2).

subroutines.name: setup_disc_write
subroutines.title: Parse filename from OSFILE block and search
subroutines.description:
Extract filename from the OSFILE control block, parse
the path, and search the current directory.

On exit:
  Z set if found, (&B6) points to entry
  Z clear if not found

subroutines.name: set_up_directory_search
subroutines.title: Validate path and check for wildcards
subroutines.description:
Scan the filename at (&B4) checking for invalid
characters and wildcards. Generates Bad name or
Wild cards errors for invalid patterns.

subroutines.name: copy_entry_from_template
subroutines.title: Copy OSFILE template into directory entry
subroutines.description:
Copy filename, attributes, and sector information from
the OSFILE workspace into the directory entry at (&B6).

subroutines.name: print_via_osasci
subroutines.title: Print character preserving registers
subroutines.description:
Write A via OSASCI while preserving A, X, and (&B6).
Used during catalogue printing.

subroutines.name: print_entry_name_and_access
subroutines.title: Print entry name and access string
subroutines.description:
Print the 10-character padded filename from (&B6)
followed by the access attribute string (R, W, L, D).

subroutines.name: verify_dir_and_list
subroutines.title: Verify directory and print catalogue header
subroutines.description:
Verify directory integrity then print directory title,
sequence number, drive number, and name as the header
for a catalogue listing.

subroutines.name: parse_path_and_load
subroutines.title: Parse path and load target directory
subroutines.description:
Parse a full pathname and load the target directory
into the buffer. Handles drive specifiers, root,
parent, and current directory references.

subroutines.name: allocate_disc_space_for_file
subroutines.title: Allocate disc space and store in entry
subroutines.description:
Allocate disc space from the FSM for the requested
file size, then store the allocated sector address
in the directory entry at (&B6).

subroutines.name: parse_and_search_dir
subroutines.title: Check next path character is terminator
subroutines.description:
Read the character at (&B4),Y and generate a Bad name
error if it is not a filename terminator.

subroutines.name: output_byte_to_buffer
subroutines.title: Output byte to Tube or host buffer
subroutines.description:
Write byte in A to the OSGBPB output destination. If Tube
is active, sends via Tube R3; otherwise stores via
(zp_mem_ptr) indirect and advances the byte counter.

subroutines.name: convert_drive_to_slot
subroutines.title: Convert drive number to slot index
subroutines.description:
Shift drive number in A right 4 bits to produce a slot
index in X.

subroutines.name: find_buffer_for_sector
subroutines.title: Find or allocate a buffer for a sector
subroutines.description:
Scan channel buffer table for a buffer matching the target
sector. If not found, evict the oldest buffer for reuse.

subroutines.name: check_drive_and_reload_fsm
subroutines.title: Check disc changed and reload FSM if needed
subroutines.description:
Read the system clock for disc-change timing, then check
whether the current drive's disc has changed since last
access. If changed, reload the FSM from disc.

subroutines.name: clear_transfer_complete
subroutines.title: Clear floppy transfer complete flag
subroutines.description: Clear bit 0 of the floppy transfer state byte.

subroutines.name: bad_compact_error
subroutines.title: Raise Bad compact error
subroutines.description: Reload FSM and directory then raise error &94: Bad compact.

subroutines.name: sync_ext_to_ptr
subroutines.title: Synchronise EXT to PTR if at EOF
subroutines.description:
If the EOF flag is set, copy PTR to EXT. Then recalculate
channel flags from the writable and open bits.

subroutines.name: store_wksp_checksum_ba_y
subroutines.title: Calculate and store workspace checksum
subroutines.description: Calculate workspace checksum and store at (zp_wksp_ptr)+&FE.

subroutines.name: load_dir_for_drive
subroutines.title: Restore workspace and load directory
subroutines.description:
Restore workspace from saved copy, then load the current
directory from disc for the active drive.

subroutines.name: calc_buffer_page_from_offset
subroutines.title: Calculate buffer page from channel offset
subroutines.description:
Divide the channel offset by 4 and add the buffer base
page (&17) to compute the buffer memory page.

subroutines.name: search_dir_for_file
subroutines.title: Search directory for matching file
subroutines.description:
Copy catalogue data from the entry at (zp_entry_ptr) and
search the current directory for a matching filename.

subroutines.name: sum_free_space
subroutines.title: Sum all free space in FSM
subroutines.description:
Walk the FSM entries accumulating the 3-byte length of
each free extent into workspace &105D-&105F.

subroutines.name: check_disc_command_type
subroutines.title: Check command type and adjust sector count
subroutines.description:
For write commands with partial transfers, round up the
sector count. For reads, skip the adjustment.

subroutines.name: parse_second_filename
subroutines.title: Parse second filename from command line
subroutines.description:
Skip past the first filename, save the text pointer, then
parse the second filename for commands like *RENAME and
*COPY. Raises Bad command if extra arguments follow.

subroutines.name: set_drive_from_channel
subroutines.title: Set current drive from channel's drive
subroutines.description:
Extract drive bits from A, check disc-change timing, and
reload the FSM if the drive's disc has changed.

subroutines.name: validate_and_set_ptr
subroutines.title: Flush buffers and set file pointer
subroutines.description:
Scan the ensure table for entries matching the current
channel and flush any dirty buffers before updating PTR.

subroutines.name: flush_all_channels
subroutines.title: Flush all open channel buffers
subroutines.description:
Iterate all channel entries, flushing dirty buffers to disc
and clearing state flags. Used by OSARGS A=&FF.

subroutines.name: setup_entry_name_ptr
subroutines.title: Set up entry name pointer for star commands
subroutines.description:
Point (zp_entry_ptr) to a pathname format string in ROM
and prepare to print up to 12 characters.

subroutines.name: setup_osgbpb_output_buffer
subroutines.title: Set up OSGBPB output buffer
subroutines.description:
Configure the output buffer for OSGBPB A=5-8. Claims the
Tube if the target address is in second processor memory.

subroutines.name: conditional_info_display
subroutines.title: Display file info if *OPT1 verbose
subroutines.description:
Check *OPT1 verbose flag. If set, print full catalogue
info for the current directory entry.

subroutines.name: transfer_sector_bytes
subroutines.title: Transfer sector bytes between buffer and memory
subroutines.description:
Copy bytes from position l10b6 to l10b7 within the
current sector buffer, routing through direct memory,
indirect via (zp_buf_dest), or the Tube.

subroutines.name: process_floppy_result
subroutines.title: Set up FDC registers and seek to track
subroutines.description:
Write track and sector to the WD1770 registers with
readback verify, then seek to the target track.

subroutines.name: exec_disc_command
subroutines.title: Execute disc command and check for error
subroutines.description:
Execute disc command via command_exec_xy. On error,
generate a BRK. On success, restore saved drive.

subroutines.name: get_drive_bit_mask
subroutines.title: Get bit mask for drive slot
subroutines.description:
Build a bit mask by rotating based on the drive slot
index, then AND with drive-change flags.

subroutines.name: output_dir_entry_name
subroutines.title: Output 10-byte directory entry name
subroutines.description:
Write name length byte then 10 characters from
(zp_text_ptr), replacing control chars with spaces.

subroutines.name: switch_to_channel_drive
subroutines.title: Switch to channel's drive for I/O
subroutines.description:
Save CSD sector and current drive, then switch to the
drive associated with the current channel.

subroutines.name: check_and_delete_found
subroutines.title: Validate and delete a directory entry
subroutines.description:
Check file is not open, verify locked attribute, for
directories confirm empty, then proceed with deletion.

subroutines.name: update_ext_from_new_ptr
subroutines.title: Update EXT from new PTR value
subroutines.description:
Copy 4-byte PTR from workspace to the channel's EXT,
then save workspace and restore drive state.

subroutines.name: increment_ptr_after_write
subroutines.title: Increment PTR after byte write
subroutines.description:
Increment the channel's 4-byte PTR. On page boundaries,
save workspace and propagate carry through mid/high bytes.

subroutines.name: next_conflict_check
subroutines.title: Continue open-channel conflict scan
subroutines.description:
Advance to next channel and continue scanning for files
that conflict with the file being opened.

subroutines.name: select_fdc_rw_command
subroutines.title: Select and issue FDC read/write command
subroutines.description:
Choose WD1770 read (&80) or write (&A0) command based
on transfer direction. Apply head load delay and step
rate, then issue the command.

subroutines.name: format_init_dir
subroutines.title: Initialise directory structure for format
subroutines.description:
Set up source and destination sector addresses for
directory initialisation during a disc format operation.

subroutines.name: setup_disc_read_for_dir
subroutines.title: Set up disc read for directory load
subroutines.description:
Copy a disc operation template to the workspace and set
up the sector address for reading a directory from disc.

subroutines.name: claim_nmi_and_init
subroutines.title: Claim NMI and initialise floppy transfer
subroutines.description:
Claim the NMI vector via service call 12, set FDC step
rate, clear error flags, and copy the NMI handler code
into NMI workspace.

subroutines.name: clear_seek_flag
subroutines.title: Clear floppy seek-in-progress flag
subroutines.description: Clear bit 1 of the floppy transfer state byte.

subroutines.name: check_existing_for_save
subroutines.title: Check for existing file before save
subroutines.description:
Search directory using wildcards for an existing entry
matching the save filename.

subroutines.name: setup_print_hex_field
subroutines.title: Calculate FSM sector checksums
subroutines.description:
Compute 8-bit checksums of FSM sectors 0 and 1 by
summing all 255 bytes of each sector.

subroutines.name: disc_op_tpl_write_fsm_unused
subroutines.title: Unused write-FSM disc operation template
subroutines.description:
An unreferenced disc operation template for writing the FSM
back to disc. The actual write-FSM code at write_dir_and_validate
instead copies the read template (disc_op_tpl_read_dir) and
patches the command byte from &08 (read) to &0A (write).
This template may be a remnant from an earlier code revision.

subroutines.name: clear_rwl_attributes
subroutines.title: Clear R, W, L attribute bits in entry
subroutines.description:
Strip bit 7 from the first three name bytes of the
directory entry at (zp_entry_ptr).

subroutines.name: print_help_command_list
subroutines.title: Print *HELP ADFS command list
subroutines.description:
Print the ADFS command list for *HELP output, formatting
each command name with padding.

subroutines.name: combine_hex_digit_pair
subroutines.title: Combine two hex nibbles into a byte
subroutines.description:
Take high nibble from workspace, shift left 4, and
OR with low nibble to produce a combined byte.

subroutines.name: ca016
subroutines.title: Print a space character
subroutines.description: Print a single space (&20) via OSWRCH.

subroutines.name: no_open_files_on_drive
subroutines.title: No open file conflict found
subroutines.description:
All channels checked with no conflicts. Continue with
the file operation.

subroutines.name: save_workspace_state
subroutines.title: Save all registers and workspace
subroutines.description:
Save registers, validate workspace checksum, check FSM
integrity, and store workspace with updated checksum.

subroutines.name: mark_saved_drive_unset
subroutines.title: Raise Wild cards error
subroutines.description: Reload FSM and directory then raise error &FD: Wild cards.

subroutines.name: broken_directory_error
subroutines.title: Raise Broken directory error
subroutines.description:
Generate disc error with state recovery, then raise
error &A8: Broken directory.

subroutines.name: check_first_char_wildcard
subroutines.title: Validate FSM map checksums
subroutines.description:
Recalculate FSM sector 0 and sector 1 checksums and
compare with stored values. Raises Bad FS map error
if either checksum does not match.

subroutines.name: retry_after_error
subroutines.title: Set up track for floppy retry
subroutines.description:
After a floppy error, set up the track for a retry
attempt by copying target sector to current track.

subroutines.name: set_read_transfer_mode
subroutines.title: Set read mode and initialise floppy
subroutines.description:
Set bit 7 of transfer mode for read, get step rate,
claim NMI, and set up the track.

subroutines.name: service4_claim_and_dispatch
subroutines.title: Decline service 4 and pass on
subroutines.description:
Clean up stack and return A=4 to pass the unrecognised
command to the next ROM in the service chain.

subroutines.name: add_size_to_existing_entry
subroutines.title: Add released size to FSM entry
subroutines.description:
Copy the object sector address and add the released
block size to an existing FSM length entry, merging
adjacent free regions.

subroutines.name: insert_new_entry
subroutines.title: Insert new entry into FSM
subroutines.description:
Check for room in the FSM. If full, raise Map full error.
Otherwise shift entries up and insert the new entry at
the correct sorted position.

subroutines.name: adjust_for_partial_xfer
subroutines.title: Execute disc transfer in batches
subroutines.description:
For transfers exceeding 255 sectors, loop with full
batches. For the final batch, use the remaining count.

subroutines.name: check_both_exhausted
subroutines.title: Check pattern and name both exhausted
subroutines.description:
After pattern ends, check whether the entry name has
also ended. Returns Z set if the match succeeds.

subroutines.name: begin_star_match
subroutines.title: Begin wildcard '*' matching
subroutines.description:
Skip past '*' in pattern and try matching the rest
against each successive position in the entry name.

subroutines.name: star_match_succeeded
subroutines.title: Return successful wildcard match
subroutines.description: Set A=0 and carry to signal a successful match.

subroutines.name: check_name_ended
subroutines.title: Check name ended during '*' match
subroutines.description:
After name is exhausted, check whether remaining pattern
is only terminators. Returns Z set if match succeeds.

subroutines.name: bad_drive_name
subroutines.title: Raise Bad name error for invalid drive
subroutines.description:
Jump to bad name error handler for an invalid drive
specifier character.

subroutines.name: set_file_attributes
subroutines.title: Set file attributes from access string
subroutines.description:
Clear existing R, W, L attributes then parse the access
string to set appropriate flags including E and D.

subroutines.name: adfs_hardware_found
subroutines.title: Claim workspace for ADFS
subroutines.description:
Return A=1 to claim one workspace page and set Y=&1C
to raise PAGE to &1D00 for ADFS workspace.

subroutines.name: wait_write_data_phase
subroutines.title: Write 256 bytes to SCSI bus
subroutines.description:
Transfer a page from (zp_buf_src) to the SCSI data
register, then set the ensuring flag.

subroutines.name: read_single_hd_sector
subroutines.title: Read a single sector via SCSI
subroutines.description:
Issue a single-sector read command and transfer 256
bytes from the SCSI data register into the buffer.

subroutines.name: step_ensure_offset_loop
subroutines.title: Step through ensure table entries
subroutines.description:
Step backward through the ensure table checking for
entries associated with the current channel.

subroutines.name: eof_error
subroutines.title: Raise EOF error
subroutines.description: Clear EOF and buffer flags then raise error &DF: EOF.

subroutines.name: calc_bget_sector_addr
subroutines.title: Calculate sector address for BGET
subroutines.description:
Compute disc sector from channel base + PTR, load the
sector into the buffer, and set up the byte offset.

subroutines.name: advance_to_next_dir_entry
subroutines.title: Advance directory scan pointer
subroutines.description:
Add 26 bytes to the directory entry pointer to move to
the next entry, handling page crossing.

subroutines.name: update_ext_to_ptr
subroutines.title: Handle PTR exceeding EXT
subroutines.description:
If PTR has exceeded the file allocation, begin file
extension. Otherwise jump to EOF write handler.

subroutines.name: update_dir_entry_on_close
subroutines.title: Update directory entry on file close
subroutines.description:
Switch to the file's drive, calculate actual sectors
used from EXT, then release unused allocation back to
the free space map.

subroutines.name: str_at
subroutines.title: Error suffix string constants
subroutines.description:
Reversed string constants used when building error messages.
str_at contains ': ta ' (reversed ' at :') appended to disc
error messages, and str_on_channel contains ' lennahc no '
(reversed ' on channel') for channel-specific errors.

subroutines.name: str_exec_abbrev
subroutines.title: OSCLI abbreviation strings
subroutines.description:
CR-terminated command abbreviation strings passed to OSCLI:
str_exec_abbrev = 'E.' (*EXEC), str_spool_abbrev = 'SP.'
(*SPOOL). Also includes str_yes (reversed 'YES' + CR for
*DESTROY confirmation) and str_hugo (NUL + 'Hugo' directory
identity string).

subroutines.name: disc_op_tpl_read_fsm
subroutines.title: Disc operation templates for FSM and directory reads
subroutines.description:
Two overlapping disc operation control block templates that
share common fields. The templates are copied to workspace
&1014-&101F before issuing disc read commands.

disc_op_tpl_read_fsm (&880C, 10 bytes via l8816+offset):
  Read 2 sectors from sector 0 into &0E00 (FSM buffer).
  Used to reload the free space map from disc.

disc_op_tpl_read_dir (&8817, 11 bytes):
  Read 5 sectors from sector 2 into &1200 (directory buffer).
  Used to load a directory from disc.

The templates overlap at &8817-&881B, sharing the result
byte (&01), host memory marker (&FFFF), and read command
(&08). The zero byte at l8816 (&8816) provides padding
when copying starts from &1014 instead of &1015.

subroutines.name: osfile_dispatch_lo
subroutines.title: OSFILE dispatch table
subroutines.description:
RTS-trick dispatch table for OSFILE functions 0-7. Low
bytes at &9269, high bytes at &926A, interleaved as pairs.
Functions: 0=save, 1=write cat info, 2=write load addr,
3=write exec addr, 4=write attrs, 5=read cat info,
6=delete, 7=create.

subroutines.name: l9316
subroutines.title: Access attribute character table
subroutines.description:
Five-character table 'RWLDE' used to look up and display
file access attributes. Indexed by attribute bit position.

subroutines.name: default_workspace_data
subroutines.title: Default CSD and library workspace data
subroutines.description:
Default values for the CSD and library workspace: two
10-byte directory names (both '$' padded with spaces)
followed by two 4-byte sector addresses (both sector 2).
Copied to workspace during initialisation.

subroutines.name: boot_data
subroutines.title: Auto-boot data and command strings
subroutines.description:
Boot option data (3 bytes) and CR-terminated OSCLI command
strings for auto-boot: 'L.$.!BOOT' (load option) and
'E.$.!BOOT' (exec option).

subroutines.name: service_dispatch_lo
subroutines.title: Service call dispatch table
subroutines.description:
RTS-trick dispatch table for MOS service calls 0-9.
Low bytes at &9A8F, high bytes at &9A99, 10 entries.

subroutines.name: tbl_extended_vectors
subroutines.title: Extended vector table
subroutines.description:
Extended vector entries for FILEV, ARGSV, BGETV, BPUTV,
and GBPBV. Each entry is a 3-byte record: address low,
address high, ROM number. Installed when ADFS is selected
as the current filing system.

subroutines.name: str_filing_system_name
subroutines.title: Filing system name string
subroutines.description:
The string 'adfs' (reversed for stack-based comparison)
used to identify the filing system during service call
handling.

subroutines.name: fscv_dispatch_lo
subroutines.title: FSCV dispatch table
subroutines.description:
RTS-trick dispatch table for filing system control calls
0-8. Low bytes at &9E6D, high bytes at &9E76, 9 entries.
FSC 0=*OPT, 1=check EOF, 2=*/, 3=*command, 4=*RUN,
5=*CAT, 6=new FS, 7=handle range, 8=*command (OS 1.20).

subroutines.name: tbl_commands
subroutines.title: Star command name and dispatch table
subroutines.description:
Table of ADFS star command names with dispatch addresses.
Each entry is: command name bytes (bit 7 set on last),
dispatch address high, dispatch address low, parameter
count. Commands include ACCESS, BACK, BYE, CDIR, CLOSE,
COMPACT, COPY, DELETE, DESTROY, DIR, DISMOUNT, EX, FREE,
INFO, LCAT, LEX, LIB, MAP, MOUNT, REMOVE, RENAME, TITLE.

subroutines.name: nmi_code_start
subroutines.title: NMI handler code (copied to &0D00)
subroutines.description:
NMI handler routines for floppy disc data transfer. This
code is copied from ROM to the NMI workspace at &0D00
at the start of each floppy operation. Handles byte-by-byte
transfer between the WD1770 data register and memory,
with variants for direct memory, Tube read, and Tube write.

subroutines.name: str_rom_footer
subroutines.title: ROM footer text
subroutines.description:
The text 'and Hugo.' followed by CR. This fills the last
10 bytes of the ROM, a signature referencing the Hugo
directory format used by ADFS.

subroutines.name: tbl_forbidden_chars
subroutines.title: Forbidden filename characters
subroutines.description:
Six characters that may not appear in ADFS filenames because
they have special meaning in the pathname syntax. The path
validator at set_up_directory_search loops through this table,
rejecting any filename containing these characters.

subroutines.name: check_file_not_open2
subroutines.title: Copy OSFILE addresses and search for empty entry
subroutines.description:
Copy the load and exec addresses from the OSFILE control
block into the disc operation workspace, then search the
current directory for an empty entry slot to use for a
new file. Called when creating files via OSFILE save,
*CDIR, *RENAME, and *COPY.

subroutines.name: osfile_save_check_existing
subroutines.title: OSFILE A=0: check for existing file before save
subroutines.description:
Entry point for OSFILE save (A=0), reached via RTS-trick
dispatch from my_osfile. Searches the current directory for
an existing file with the same name, checking it is not a
directory and has the correct access attributes.

On entry:

comments_before:
Disc operation templates for FSM and directory reads

Two overlapping disc operation control block templates that
share common fields. The templates are copied to workspace
&1014-&101F before issuing disc read commands.

disc_op_tpl_read_fsm (&880C, 10 bytes via l8816+offset):
  Read 2 sectors from sector 0 into &0E00 (FSM buffer).
  Used to reload the free space map from disc.

disc_op_tpl_read_dir (&8817, 11 bytes):
  Read 5 sectors from sector 2 into &1200 (directory buffer).
  Used to load a directory from disc.

The templates overlap at &8817-&881B, sharing the result
byte (&01), host memory marker (&FFFF), and read command
(&08). The zero byte at l8816 (&8816) provides padding
when copying starts from &1014 instead of &1015.
